learning_outcomes.description: Deepen understanding and mastery of CBT skills
Apply deliberate practice strategies to improve therapeutic skill development
Engage in reflective and reflexive practice, as well as self-care
Manage process issues in therapy (e.g., working to repair therapeutic ruptures and working with shame)
Able to apply CBT with culturally and linguistically diverse populations
Able to continue to apply the scientist-practitioner model to your therapy practice.

assessments.due_date:
1/09/2026

Students complete an in person, closed book, online quiz. It will be 20 MCQ questions covering the first five weeks of the semester. Students will have 40 minutes to complete it.
assessments.task_description:
Students complete an in person, closed book, online quiz. It will be 20 MCQ questions covering the first five weeks of the semester. Students will have 40 minutes to complete it.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: It will be completed in-person in the lecture
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
First video (two specific CBT skills) 21/08/2026

Second video (two specific CBT skills), with reflection and practice diary 9/10/2026

Students will select two specific CBT skills (e.g., downward arrow, Socratic Questions) to video at time one, which they will submit by August 21st. They will then have 7 weeks to use deliberate practice to enhance skill development for those two chosen skills. Students will then video those two specific CBT skills again. Students will also write a reflection contrasting skill performance between the two videos, as well as including a practice diary of their deliberate practice over the 7 week period. The second video is due Oct 9th.
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
Students will select two specific CBT skills (e.g., downward arrow, Socratic Questions) to video at time one, which they will submit by August 21st. They will then have 7 weeks to use deliberate practice to enhance skill development for those two chosen skills. Students will then video those two specific CBT skills again. Students will also write a reflection contrasting skill performance between the two videos, as well as including a practice diary of their deliberate practice over the 7 week period. The second video is due Oct 9th.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission using the Turnitin link on the Blackboard assessment page
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be required to write and submit within the tutorial a self-reflection in five tutorials (2% each) about the skill role played. In total 5 self-reflections will be written and submitted within the tutorials.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person in the tutorials. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Signed off by tutor in the tutorial
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
26/10/2026

The competency test will be scheduled in the final tutorial
assessments.task_description: Students will be assigned a role play regarding managing a process issue and will be asked to prepare and demonstrate a specific therapy competency, with a fellow student role playing their client. They will also be asked to give a brief verbal self-reflection on their demonstration. A specific time schedule will be given closer to the day.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
